project_description:
1. Product Overview
1.1 Vision

Build a fully autonomous, AI-driven system that generates a complete, stylistically cohesive tarot deck (78 cards) using structured agent collaboration, multimodal evaluation, and controlled retry logic.

The system must produce commercially viable, production-ready tarot card imagery with minimal human intervention.

1.2 Problem Statement

AI image generation is powerful but inconsistent. Generating 78 cohesive tarot cards manually leads to:

Style drift

Symbol inconsistency

Anatomy errors

Text artifacts

Fantasy creep

Inconsistent lighting/palette

There is currently no structured system that:

Enforces deck-wide art direction

Validates symbolic alignment

Automatically repairs failing generations

Maintains cohesion across dozens of images

This product solves that.

2. Goals & Success Metrics
2.1 Primary Goals

Generate 78 tarot cards with cohesive style

Maintain suit-level visual systems

Eliminate common AI failure artifacts

Achieve >85% pass rate within 3 retries

Require no manual prompt rewriting

2.2 Success Metrics
Metric	Target
Cards passing within 3 retries	≥ 85%
Average retries per card	≤ 2
No text/watermark artifacts	100%
Suit palette compliance	≥ 0.85 average
Major Arcana visual cohesion score	≥ 0.85
3. User Persona

Primary User:

Indie tarot deck creator

Spiritual brand founder

Digital product entrepreneur

AI-native creative technologist

Secondary User:

Publishing studio

Art director building variant decks

AI art platform operator

4. Product Scope
4.1 In Scope (MVP)

CrewAI-based multi-agent system

YAML-driven configuration

Sequential Flow orchestration

Style Bible generation

Per-card concept + prompt generation

Image generation via OpenAI

Multimodal evaluation

Prompt repair + retry loop

Results manifest output

4.2 Out of Scope (Phase 1)

UI dashboard

Marketplace publishing

Human approval gates

Multi-deck batch scaling

Custom training models

5. Functional Requirements
5.1 Style Bible Generation
Description

Generate a structured Deck Style Bible from user creative direction.

Requirements

Output strict JSON

Define:

Global style rules

Suit-specific visual systems

Major Arcana rules

Prompt prefix + avoid constraints

Include guardrails for:

No text/logos

No extra limbs

No watermark

Minimal background

Acceptance Criteria

JSON validates against schema

All four suits have distinct palettes + lighting

Global avoid rules exist

No markdown in output

5.2 Concept Generation
Description

Generate structured, filmable visual concept for a card.

Requirements

Max 1–3 symbolic elements

Enforce suit rules (Minor)

Enforce archetypal realism (Major)

No fantasy effects unless allowed

Minimal background complexity

JSON only output

Acceptance Criteria

JSON matches schema

Symbol budget ≤ 3

Suit rules referenced in style_notes

No prompt string generated at this stage

5.3 Prompt Construction
Description

Convert concept JSON into one production-ready prompt string.

Requirements

Must start with global_prefix

Must include suit_prefix OR major_prefix

Must end with global_avoid

Must not introduce new symbolism

Must be one continuous string

Acceptance Criteria

No markdown

No JSON

No bullet formatting

Contains required prefix sections

5.4 Image Generation
Description

Generate a single image using OpenAI image model.

Requirements

Model: gpt-image-1

Size: 1024x1536

PNG output

Exactly one image per attempt

No extra objects beyond prompt

Acceptance Criteria

Returns valid image URL/reference

No multiple images

No additional commentary

5.5 Multimodal Evaluation
Description

Evaluate image against concept + style bible.

Requirements

Must score:

Suit compliance

Global style adherence

Meaning alignment

Image quality

Must detect:

Text artifacts

Watermarks

Extra limbs

Hand distortions

Palette drift

Fantasy drift

Background clutter

Must output structured JSON with:

pass boolean

subscores

rule_checks

reasons

prompt_patch

failure_mode

Pass Criteria

Suit ≥ 0.80

Global style ≥ 0.80

Meaning ≥ 0.80

Quality ≥ 0.75

No critical violations

5.6 Prompt Repair
Description

Revise prompt when evaluation fails.

Requirements

Apply replace → remove → add

Escalate simplification by attempt count

Preserve meaning

Do not introduce new symbolism

Output prompt string only

Escalation Logic
Attempt	Behavior
1	Surgical edits only
2	Reduce background complexity
3+	Safe mode: single subject, minimal background, 1 symbol
5.7 Sequential Flow
Description

Orchestrate crews per card.

Flow Steps

Generate Style Bible

For each card:

Generate concept

Build prompt

Generate image

Evaluate

Repair if needed

Retry until pass or max_retries

Store best attempt

Output results manifest

Acceptance Criteria

Style Bible generated once

Cards processed sequentially

max_retries respected

Best attempt preserved

6. Data Structures
6.1 Card Spec Input
{
  "card_id": "SWORDS_ACE",
  "card_name": "Ace of Swords",
  "arcana": "Minor",
  "suit": "Swords",
  "meaning_keywords": ["clarity", "truth", "decision"]
}
6.2 Results Manifest Output
{
  "SWORDS_ACE": {
    "concept": {...},
    "initial_prompt": "...",
    "best_attempt": {
      "attempt": 2,
      "image_ref": "...",
      "eval": {...},
      "status": "passed"
    }
  }
}
7. Non-Functional Requirements

Deterministic structure where possible

Modular crew separation

YAML-configurable agents

Retry logic must not drift style

No copyrighted artist references

Cost efficiency awareness

8. Risks & Mitigation
Risk	Mitigation
Anatomy failures	Escalation safe mode
Style drift	Style Bible as contract
Symbol creep	Symbol budget limit
Fantasy escalation	Hard rule enforcement
Parsing failures	Strict JSON-only tasks
9. Future Enhancements

Human approval checkpoints

Parallel batch mode

Deck variation generator

Cost optimizer mode

Variant art styles per deck theme

UI dashboard

10. Definition of Done

The product is complete when:

78 cards can be generated sequentially

≥85% pass within 3 retries

No text/watermark artifacts

Manifest file generated

System runs without manual prompt rewriting
